summary: 7W / 8L across all 15 matchups (47% hit rate on model directional picks). The only real P&L exposure was CHW @ TEX — Claude's sole OVERRIDE of HARD CAP 7's suppression, justified by citing deGrom as an offsetting ace — which lost 10-3 after deGrom allowed 5 ER in 4.2 IP, directly contradicting the override's own rationale. The model's 7 GOLD-tier picks went 4-3 directionally (57%), but Claude's intervention pattern was net-negative: 4 DOWNGRADEs suppressed real wins (BAL@BOS, NYM@MIL, WSH@COL, STL@LAA) while the one OVERRIDE landed on the loser. Two DOWNGRADEs (SF@KC, OAK@ARI) were correctly protective. The starring-F1-without-F3 auto-DOWNGRADE rule went 0-for-2 today and needs re-evaluation.

MIN @ CLE.headline: CLE routed MIN 13-4; CONFIRM SKIP correctly withheld stake on a bottom-bucket marginal favorite that got blown out.
MIN @ CLE.hypothesis: HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0242, sitting above the sub-0.02 false-negative band where the disputed pattern lives — a legitimate cap fire, not a misfire. CAP 10 also independently zeroed the bottom-bucket pick (pick_prob=0.534). CLE's 9-run margin validates both gates. No process failure; MIN was correctly treated as an unstakeable coin-flip.
MIN @ CLE.signals_to_recheck: HARD CAP 7 and HARD CAP 10 — both correctly fired here (gap=0.0242 above the disputed <0.02 band); this is NOT a false negative to track alongside the sub-0.02 pattern.

PIT @ NYY.headline: NYY beat PIT 8-5; dual-opposing D-grade SKIP was correctly zero-staked, and this time the directional read was also right.
PIT @ NYY.hypothesis: SP-xERA (1.11 against) and PQI (-17.6 against) both opposed NYY (grade D, score -2), yet NYY won — consistent with the settled archive rule that dual-opposing is stake-suppression only, not a fade. SKIP was correct regardless of direction; the directional WIN here is noise relative to the sizing rule. Variance, not process.
PIT @ NYY.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap/pqi_diff dual-opposing at grade D — correctly zeroed the stake; directional accuracy was right this time but remains close-to-even across the archive, confirming suppression-only rule.

TB @ TOR.headline: TB blew out TOR 7-1; CONFIRM SKIP correctly avoided a HARD CAP 1 + Stage1/2 disagreement pick that lost badly.
TB @ TOR.hypothesis: f5_full_delta=0.386 was the slate's largest Stage1/2 split (Cease F5-dominant, near-even full-game), and HARD CAP 1 had already zeroed the negative -5.16pp edge (score 3→1). TOR's 6-run loss validates the suppression cleanly. The large delta flagged genuine model disagreement about the pick's stability — process success.
TB @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta and [HARD CAP 1] negative-edge GOLD — a 0.386 Stage1/2 split plus negative edge correctly flagged instability; TOR's blowout loss supports treating |delta|>0.25 as a hard stake-suppression trigger.

BAL @ BOS.headline: BOS edged BAL 6-5; the starred-F1-without-F3 DOWNGRADE missed a real A-grade win for the first time in the tracked streak.
BAL @ BOS.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate. F1_xera_gap=1.39* fired with no F3 corroboration, triggering a DOWNGRADE under a rule the archive had validated 4+ consecutive times. But BOS won by 1 run on a genuine pre-cap A score-5 stack, and SP-xERA (Shane Baz, 1.34 edge) independently agreed with the BOS pick — meaning the SP-xERA signal is at least partially independent of the starred F1, not purely redundant as the rule assumes. Archive moves to 4-for-6 on this shape; the rule may need a carve-out when SP-xERA gap is also large on the same side.
BAL @ BOS.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap* without F3_swing_take_gap — broke its streak today (BOS won 6-5 after DOWNGRADE); archive now 4W/2L on this rule shape. Consider whether a large SP-xERA gap (>1.0) on the same side as a starred F1 constitutes partial independent corroboration.

LAD @ PHI.headline: PHI beat LAD 10-7; clean dual-opposing D-grade SKIP was correctly sized, and the opposing read was also directionally right.
LAD @ PHI.hypothesis: SP-xERA (0.69 against LAD) and PQI (+14.4 against LAD) both opposed the pick, and PHI won — the opposing read was directionally correct this time. SKIP sizing was correct regardless. No process failure; the dual-opposing rule continues to function as designed (zero-stake, not a fade).
LAD @ PHI.signals_to_recheck: None — dual-opposing correctly predicted the loser here; consistency with the suppression-only rule, not a signal to promote to directional fade.

SD @ ATL.headline: ATL edged SD 3-2; CONFIRM SKIP correct on a coin-flip pick outside the valid edge band.
SD @ ATL.hypothesis: Edge -8.31pp sat outside the [4,15]pp valid window and PQI leaned slightly against ATL (Δ=-3.3). CAP 10 zeroed the marginal favorite at pick_prob=0.508. ATL's 1-run win is consistent with a correctly-suppressed near-coin-flip, not a missed signal. Neither process success nor process failure — variance on a correctly-untouched game.
SD @ ATL.signals_to_recheck: None — edge outside acceptable band plus CAP 10 bottom-bucket correctly suppressed a coin-flip outcome.

SF @ KC.headline: KC beat SF 4-3; DOWNGRADE of the PLATINUM-demoted/CAP10-zeroed GOLD pick was correct and protective.
SF @ KC.hypothesis: The PLATINUM→GOLD guardrail had already demoted SF (rolling hit rate ~43%), and Stage1/2 disagreed by 0.27 (Wacha F5-heavy vs a near-even full game), adding real instability. CAP 10 zeroed the marginal-favorite score to 0 after the demotion. SF's 1-run loss validates all three layers of suppression — a clean process success.
SF @ KC.signals_to_recheck: PLATINUM guardrail demotion plus Stage1/2 disagreement (f5_full_delta=0.2675) and HARD CAP 10 — three-layer suppression correctly called a losing marginal favorite.

NYM @ MIL.headline: MIL crushed NYM 8-3; the negative-edge DOWNGRADE (CAP 1) missed a 5-run GOLD win with a strong pre-cap A- stack.
NYM @ MIL.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate. HARD CAP 1 fired on the -3.19pp edge, correctly capping on paper, but the underlying pre-cap stack (SP-xERA +1.75, PQI +16.2, Stage agree, score 4/A-) was directionally strong and MIL won by 5 runs. This is the third consecutive instance (after TB@BOS and MIA@MIL on 7/18-7/19) of CAP 1 zeroing a pre-cap score-4+ stack that subsequently won. The cap appears too blunt when pre-cap score is ≥4, even on a slightly negative edge.
NYM @ MIL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD — zeroed a pre-cap A- score-4 stack (MIL won 8-3); now 3 consecutive occurrences of CAP 1 suppressing a score-4+ winner (TB@BOS 7/19, MIA@MIL 7/18-7/19, NYM@MIL today). Consider a CAP 1 carve-out when pre-cap score ≥4.

DET @ CHC.headline: DET beat CHC 8-6; dual-opposing D-grade SKIP correctly zero-staked, and the opposing read was directionally right.
DET @ CHC.hypothesis: SP-xERA (1.18 against CHC) and PQI (-11.0 against CHC) both opposed the pick, and CHC lost — the opposing read called the right direction. SKIP was correctly sized at zero regardless. No process failure in either direction.
DET @ CHC.signals_to_recheck: None — dual-opposing correctly predicted the loser; zero-stake sizing was right.

CHW @ TEX.headline: CWS routed TEX 10-3 — the slate's only real stake (GOLD OVERRIDE of HARD CAP 7) was a clear process failure.
CHW @ TEX.hypothesis: Process failure. Claude overrode CAP 7's suppression on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0077, citing deGrom's ace innings as an offsetting factor that reduces reliance on the bullpen exposure the cap penalizes. But deGrom allowed 5 ER in 4.2 IP and took the loss, directly falsifying the core override rationale. The sub-0.02 band pattern remains net-positive in aggregate (6-of-7 tracked instances won), but converting a population-level pattern into a single real stake using a starter's reputation — without checking recent form or rest — is a category error. This is the band's first real-stake loss.
CHW @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage sub-0.02 band — first real-money stake on this pattern lost 10-3 (archive: 6-1 in sub-0.02 band but 0-1 when converted to real stake); do not cite a starter's reputation as offsetting-factor justification without checking recent ERA/IP before staking.

MIA @ HOU.headline: HOU beat MIA 8-5; sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO correctly withheld the stake on Blanco's 0-Statcast-pitch current-season sample.
MIA @ HOU.hypothesis: Ronel Blanco (career 45 GS / 3.45 ERA) triggered the sp_savant_gate HARD_VETO solely because of his 0 Statcast pitches tracked this season — a fresh return from injury/rehab that creates a current-season data void even without inexperience. HOU winning doesn't argue against the veto; it's a data-availability gate that functions independently of the directional outcome. Process success.
MIA @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO on thin_sp_data (0 current-season Statcast pitches) — functioned correctly; career track record does not override the current-season data gate.

WSH @ COL.headline: WSH throttled COL 7-3; the triple-stacked DOWNGRADE (CAP1 + CAP10 + starred-F1-no-F3) missed a comfortable 4-run GOLD win.
WSH @ COL.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate and paired with BAL@BOS makes the starred-F1-without-F3 rule 0-for-2 today. WSH's F1_xera_gap=1.50* had no F3 but did have F2_xwoba_gap=0.025 and F5_bullpen_gap=+1.05, and the underlying pre-cap stack (score 4, A-) delivered comfortably. Both of today's starred-F1 misses (BAL@BOS, WSH@COL) had F2/F5 support even without F3 — the rule may need a secondary-signal carve-out rather than treating F3 as the only valid corroboration.
WSH @ COL.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap* without F3_swing_take_gap — second miss today (WSH won 7-3 after DOWNGRADE, archive now 4-2 on this shape); both of today's misses had F2/F5 secondary support — consider whether F2_xwoba_gap or F5_bullpen_gap should qualify as valid corroboration alongside F3.

OAK @ ARI.headline: OAK beat ARI 5-2; DOWNGRADE on the suspected CAP1 bypass plus rookie-SP blindspot (Bratt, 2nd career start) was correct and protective.
OAK @ ARI.hypothesis: Process success. Two independent reasons converged on the DOWNGRADE: (1) edge_pp=-3.17 is negative on a GOLD pick yet [HARD CAP 1] never appears in grade_reasons — a likely code-path bypass that Claude correctly identified and applied manually; (2) ARI's home SP Mitch Bratt had only 2 career MLB starts (6.00 ERA) — a textbook rookie-SP blindspot on the picked side. Bratt allowed 8 hits and 2 HRs in 4.2 IP, validating both flags.
OAK @ ARI.signals_to_recheck: small_sample_SP / rookie-SP blindspot (<6 career starts) confirmed on the picked side (Bratt: 8H/2HR/4.2IP); also flag for engineering: negative-edge GOLD (edge_pp=-3.17) reached Claude without a [HARD CAP 1] line in grade_reasons — a code-path gap distinct from the CAP 7 threshold debate.

CIN @ SEA.headline: SEA shut out CIN 8-0; the slate's weakest D-grade stack (score -4) correctly called the loser in emphatic fashion.
CIN @ SEA.hypothesis: SP-xERA (0.89 against), PQI (+10.3 against), Stage1/2 disagreement (delta=0.19), and fair_prob=0.397 below the 0.42 viability floor all opposed CIN — the most complete opposing signal configuration on the slate. An 8-0 shutout validates every gate cleanly. Process success.
CIN @ SEA.signals_to_recheck: None — fair_prob<0.42 floor plus dual-opposing SP/PQI and Stage1/2 disagreement produced the slate's most emphatic correct SKIP call.

STL @ LAA.headline: LAA edged STL 3-2; the conservative DOWNGRADE (choosing not to extend the CAP 7 override here as with CHW@TEX) still missed a narrow win.
STL @ LAA.hypothesis: Claude explicitly declined to override CAP 7 on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0079 because this game stacked bullpen_fatigue and a 15.06pp edge near the outer band with no offsetting ace factor — unlike CHW@TEX. LAA won by 1, a margin consistent with real uncertainty. Combined with CHW@TEX losing 10-3, the within-slate differentiation pointed the wrong way on both: the overridden game lost big, the left-suppressed game won narrow. At the game level, this is close to variance; across both together, it is a net-negative day for CAP 7 override logic.
STL @ LAA.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage sub-0.02 band — sub-0.02 archive is now 6-1 (STL@LAA won 3-2 while suppressed, CHW@TEX lost 10-3 while overridden); the population-level pattern remains net-positive but first real-stake conversion was a loss — do not override without >= 0.02 threshold fix first.

patterns_observed: Claude's tier decisions were collectively net-negative today despite individually defensible reasoning: the model's 7 GOLD picks went 4-3 directionally (57%), but Claude DOWNGRADEd 6 to SKIP (suppressing all 4 winners: BAL@BOS, NYM@MIL, WSH@COL, STL@LAA) and OVERRODe the 7th (CHW@TEX) into a real stake that lost 10-3. Going forward, default posture on GOLD picks should lean CONFIRM unless a specific rule has a recent track record ≥70% on that exact shape — today shows that stacking plausible-sounding suppression rules can erase a tier that was directionally beating the market.

The starred-F1_xera_gap-without-F3-corroboration auto-DOWNGRADE rule went 0-for-2 today (BAL@BOS BOS won 6-5; WSH@COL WSH won 7-3), breaking a streak described as '4+ consecutive validations.' Archive moves to 4W/2L on this shape. Critically, both of today's misses had F2_xwoba_gap or F5_bullpen_gap secondary support even without F3 — the rule should be revised to accept F2 or F5 as valid corroboration, not only F3, and demote to half-Kelly rather than a full SKIP pending 30+ data points on the revised shape.

[HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD prevention has now suppressed three consecutive pre-cap score-4+ GOLD stacks that went on to win (TB@BOS 7/19 pre-cap A-, MIA@MIL 7/18-19 pre-cap A-, NYM@MIL today pre-cap A-). The cap is too blunt when the underlying stack before the edge adjustment is ≥4 and at least two F-series/SP/PQI signals independently agree — consider a carve-out: when pre-cap score ≥4, treat the cap as a Kelly-damping rather than a full SKIP.

The CHW@TEX OVERRIDE (the first real-money stake on the HARD CAP 7 sub-0.02-band pattern) lost 10-3 after deGrom allowed 5 ER in 4.2 IP, directly negating the override rationale that cited deGrom's innings as offsetting the bullpen exposure. Rule going forward: a starting pitcher's reputation does not qualify as an offsetting factor for CAP 7 overrides without a concrete check of recent ERA, IP/start, and rest; population-level patterns must not be converted to single-game real stakes via qualitative logic that hasn't been independently verified.
